title: LRRK2 cloning, plasmid construction, and mutagenesis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"f0dlc","text":"Protocol for Cloning, plasmid construction, and mutagenesis of LRRK2 and LRRK2-RCKW as done by Leschziner and Reck-Peterson Labs.\n\n","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5fdhl","text":"Original protocol by David Snead  and Yu Xuan Lin.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"bpo0i","text":"Materials","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"99m2d","text":"Q5 Site-Directed Mutagenesis (NEB)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"d1vat","text":"DH5α competent cells.","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8ukqd","text":"QIAprep Spin Miniprep Kit (Qiagen)","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"5eqse","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b3a19","text":"Equipment","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":9}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"b0aoc","text":"Thermocycler","type":"unordered-list-item","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"e8jck","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: The DNA coding for LRRK2-RCKW residues 1327 to 2527 (taken from Mammalian Gene Collection) was PCR-amplified using the forward primer TACTTCCAATCCATGAAAA491AGGCTGTGCCTTATAACCGA and the reverse primer TATCCACCTTTACTGTCACTCAACAGATGTTCGTCTCATTTTTTCA. The DNA coding for LRRK2 was codon-optimized for Spodoptera frugipera (Sf9) cells and synthesized by Epoch Life Science.
The DNA for either LRRK2 and LRRK2-RCKW, containing a N-terminal His6-Z-tag and TEV protease cleavage site, was cloned into a pFB-6HZB vector (SGC) by ligation-independent cloning, RRID:Addgene_53641
LRRK2 variants were generated using Q5 Site-Directed Mutagenesis Kit (NEB).

Primers for G2019S mutant: Forward: gccaagatcgctgactacagcattgcccagtactgttgc Reversed: gcaacagtactgggcaatgctgtagtcagcgatcttggc Primers for I2020T mutant: Forward: ccaagatcgctgactacggaactgcccagtact Reversed: agtactgggcagttccgtagtcagcgatcttgg Using the following thermocycler conditions. 98 °C 00:00:30 ( 98 °C 00:00:10 50-72 °C 00:00:30 72 °C 00:13:00 72 °C 00:02:00 ) X25 cycles 4 °C Hold.
PCR products were subject to a KLD reaction (NEB), followed by a transformation into DH5α competent cells and plated on LB with antibiotics.
Colonies were picked and grown overnight for DNA extraction using Qiagen miniprep kit.

Extracted DNA plasmids were submitted for sequencing. The resulting plasmids were utilized for the generation of recombinant Baculoviruses according to the Bac-to-Bac expression system protocol (Invitrogen).
creator: Amalia Villagran Suarez
